content.text:
<user_query>
the background color of the view on line 192 of @components/BottomTabBar.tsx is transparent. however, when i scroll down on the product grid, the transparent background hides the product grid even though its not supposed to.
</user_query>

Tracing layout and stacking: reading `BottomTabBar.tsx` around line 192 and how it's composed with the product grid on the Discover screen.

[REDACTED]

Identifying the issue: Figma leaves the tab bar's top wedge unfilled so scroll content shows through, but the tab bar is laid out after the ScrollView in a column—so the wedge sits over the screen's navy background, not the scrolling grid. Positioning the tab bar absolutely so the ScrollView extends full height and increasing bottom spacing so content can scroll under the wedge (and clear the opaque chrome).

[REDACTED]

Overlaying the tab bar on the scroll area so the masked wedge reveals scrolling content (matching Figma). The column layout placed the tab bar below the ScrollView, so the "transparent" wedge only revealed the static screen background—not the grid.

[REDACTED]

        style={{
          position: 'absolute',
          left:      0,
          right:     0,
          bottom:    0,
          zIndex:    10,
        }}
      />

input.description: Typecheck and Expo lint with PowerShell separators

content.text:
Here is what was going wrong.

### Cause

`BottomTabBar` is built so the **top wedge** is empty in the SVG (masked out) so **whatever is behind the bar in z-order** shows through, as in Figma (see the comment in `BottomTabBar.tsx` around the mask and `TAB_TOP_CLEAR_WEDGE`).

On Discover, the tab bar was a **normal flex sibling under** the `ScrollView`. The scroll view’s frame stopped at the top of the bar, so behind that wedge you only ever saw the **screen background** (`COLORS.bg`), not the scrolling grid. A transparent `backgroundColor` on the wrapper does not fix that—it only means that view doesn’t paint its own fill; it still sits where there is **no** scroll content underneath.

### Fix

1. **Overlay** the nav with `position: 'absolute'`, `left` / `right` / `bottom: 0`, and `zIndex`, so the main `ScrollView` can extend under the bar and the wedge can actually sit over **live** scroll content.

2. **Bottom inset** for scroll content: replace the fixed `120 * scale` spacer with `tabBarLayoutPx + 16 * scale` (same `tabBarLayoutPx` you already use for the accent clip) so the last rows clear the **opaque** tab chrome and home inset when scrolled to the end.

Changes are in `app/index.tsx` only; line 192 in `BottomTabBar.tsx` can stay transparent—the important part is **what sits under** that layer on the Discover screen.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
<user_query>
the mountain and accessory icons have to be transparent like this Implement this design from Figma.
@https://www.figma.com/design/VYIhA7NSt2WzRJEzZNSeNY/Design-To-Ship--Copy---Copy-?node-id=1-61&m=dev and the other icons have to be more opaque and have a drop shadow like this Implement this design from Figma.
@https://www.figma.com/design/VYIhA7NSt2WzRJEzZNSeNY/Design-To-Ship--Copy---Copy-?node-id=1-53&m=dev
</user_query>

Reviewing `CategoryPill` and category icons, then aligning them with Figma nodes 1:53 and 1:61.

[REDACTED]

Applying Figma 1:53: solid inactive pills get a white border and dual shadows (already present); icons use higher opacity. Applying Figma 1:61: frosted pills (Mountain, Accessory) use lower-opacity icon strokes.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what was updated in `CategoryPill.tsx` to match the two Figma references.

### [1:61 Mountain / glass row](https://www.figma.com/design/VYIhA7NSt2WzRJEzZNSeNY/Design-To-Ship--Copy---Copy-?node-id=1-61&m=dev) — Mountain & Accessory (`frosted`)
Icons and labels use **`rgba(255,255,255,0.4)`** instead of solid white so they read as light, translucent glyphs on the blur + tint (same idea as the dev-mode asset for Mountain).

### [1:53 Road — solid inactive](https://www.figma.com/design/VYIhA7NSt2WzRJEzZNSeNY/Design-To-Ship--Copy---Copy-?node-id=1-53&m=dev) — All, Electric, Road
- **Pill:** `rgba(255,255,255,0.42)` **hairline border** (scaled, at least hairline width), on top of the existing R166 gradient and the same **upper glow + lower drop** you already had (`glowWrap` / `dropBelow`), aligned with the dual shadow + border treatment in dev mode.
- **Icons:** **`rgba(255,255,255,0.92)`** so they read clearly opaque vs the old `COLORS.textLo` (~60% white).
- **Icon shadow (iOS):** small **`#10141c`** shadow under the glyph via a wrapper `View` (and the same shadow on the **“All”** label so text matches the icon treatment). Android keeps the pill’s `elevation` only; nested SVG shadows are unreliable there without hacks.

`index.tsx` is unchanged: Mountain and Accessory still use `frosted={i >= CATEGORIES.length - 2}`.

[REDACTED]
